title: Matter & Materials
objective: Students will explore matter & materials concepts including Changing Shapes of Solids, Choosing the Right Material.

steps.content: Today we're going to learn about Changing Shapes of Solids. Get ready for an adventure!
Investigate how the shapes of solid objects can be changed by squashing, bending, twisting, and stretching
Time to try this out: Demonstrate four ways to change the shape of a solid: squashing, bending, twisting, stretching
Can you show you how to change the shape of clay by squashing it, twist a pipe cleaner, and explain that some materials bend while others snap?

Great job! Today we learned about Changing Shapes of Solids. Demonstrate four ways to change the shape of a solid: squashing, bending, twisting, stretching Compare how different materials respond to these forces (e.g. clay squashes easily, wood does not)
Let's discover the amazing world of Choosing the Right Material!
Identify and compare the suitability of everyday materials for particular uses, explaining why specific materials are chosen for specific purposes
Let's practice together! Explain why a specific material is chosen for a purpose based on its properties (e.g. glass for windows because it's transparent)
Can you explain why windows are made of glass and not wood, or why wellies are made of rubber and not paper?

Great job! Today we learned about Choosing the Right Material. Explain why a specific material is chosen for a purpose based on its properties (e.g. glass for windows because it's transparent) Compare two materials and decide which is more suitable for a given use with reasoning
